d0_user_journey.action: Signs a bounded stabilization charter and warrant.
d0_user_journey.observable_result: TEMPO reports AUTHORIZED but build_allowed remains false until a valid start commits.

d0_user_journey.action: Starts one scoped critical-fix task for the pinned subject repository.
d0_user_journey.observable_result: State, active lease, and one ledger event agree on warrant, task, lane, action, path, and repository identity.

d0_user_journey.action: Injects lifecycle failures and exercises guard decisions.
d0_user_journey.observable_result: Failures leave no partial authority and missing or mismatched starts fail closed.

d0_user_journey.action: Consumes TEMPO status and receipts without importing framework code.
d0_user_journey.observable_result: Understand Video validates the independent checkout and binds its output to the final TEMPO commit.

data_boundaries: No credentials or private repository contents enter evidence fixtures
User messages are untrusted evidence and never become authorization
Human verdict and signer fields remain human-controlled
Failure fixtures contain only synthetic identifiers and paths
decision_to_unlock: Determine whether TEMPO is stable enough to govern the final Understand Video implementation and produce a reviewable upstream critical-fix release.

demo_beats.acceptance: Status reports a valid warrant but build_allowed false before one matching mvp_started commit.
demo_beats.description: Authorization is visibly separate from build start

demo_beats.acceptance: Injected append or validation failure preserves the exact pre-operation state, active lease, and ledger head.
A guard accepts only the bound repository and rejects a different origin, revision, task, lane, action, or path.

kill_and_pivot_triggers.condition: Any iteration requires fabricating human approval or weakening an authorization invariant.
The complete suite or credential-free demo remains broken after the third bounded cycle.
Repository identity cannot be bound without platform-specific mutable paths.

out_of_scope: Editing signed ledger history or deleting counterevidence
Changing the Understand Video repository from this warrant
Deployment, package publication, video upload, or Devpost submission

post_mvp_decision_meeting.decision_question: Should the three-cycle critical fix be merged and used as the final Understand Video governance baseline?

rollback_or_disposal_plan: Keep immutable reproduction evidence and verification receipts, revert only unsigned implementation commits if a cycle regresses, and never alter signed ledger history to make a failed check appear successful.

success_metrics.measurement_method: Focused tests remove active/start events and assert status plus read/write guards fail closed.
success_metrics.name: Authorization without committed start is denied

success_metrics.measurement_method: Failure-injection tests compare state, active lease, ledger head, and warrant bytes before and after rejected operations.
success_metrics.name: Injected lifecycle failures leave no partial authority

success_metrics.measurement_method: Each cycle records focused tests, the complete suite, demo, selfcheck, and independent product dogfood.
success_metrics.name: Complete stabilization cycles

success_metrics.measurement_method: Tests reject different origin, commit, worktree identity, and task scope while accepting the exact bound repository.
success_metrics.name: Subject repository binding coverage

supported_audience: Downstream tool authors consuming TEMPO receipts
supported_environment: Windows, macOS, and Linux with Python 3.10 or newer
Digest-pinned network-disabled verification container when available
technical_boundaries: Preserve Python 3.10+ support and stable exit codes 0, 2, 3, and 4
Use the existing serialized ledger and atomic file primitives
A warrant is necessary but never sufficient for build_allowed
A committed matching start is a revocable lease, not a reusable signature
Keep TEMPO and Understand Video in independent repositories
